text.title: Laravel Umbrella Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Laravel umbrella methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: For every feature, the agent MUST route to the relevant sub-methodology: HTTP layering → `php-laravel-patterns`; ORM details → `php-eloquent`; queue work → `php-laravel-queues`; tests → `php-phpunit-testing`. Generic Laravel advice MUST NOT replace the sub-methodology's rules.
rule.rationale: Umbrella methodologies must dispatch; pretending to cover everything in one file misses the testable detail per sub-area.

rule.statement: `composer.json` MUST require PHP `^8.2`. Laravel `^10.0` minimum (`^11.0` preferred). Targeting PHP < 8.2 or Laravel < 10 is forbidden in new code.
rule.rationale: Readonly properties, enums, `Attribute::make` and current security patches require PHP 8.1+ / Laravel 10+; older targets miss critical fixes.

rule.statement: Production environments MUST set `QUEUE_CONNECTION` to a real driver (`redis` / `database` / `sqs`). `sync` is forbidden in any `*-prod`, `*-staging` env.
rule.rationale: `sync` executes inline, blocking the request and silently inverting the latency contract.

rule.statement: `.env` MUST live outside VCS. Code MUST read secrets via `config('service.key')` (config file references `env()`) — direct `env()` calls inside services are forbidden because `config:cache` freezes empty values.
rule.rationale: `env()` inside services returns null after `config:cache` runs in production deploy.

rule.statement: `php artisan schedule:work` and `php artisan queue:work` MUST run under Supervisor (or systemd) with auto-restart on failure. Cron-based `schedule:run` MUST be the single entry every minute (never duplicated per task).
rule.rationale: Crashed workers without supervisor silently stop processing; per-task cron entries multiply concurrency and break locks.
